prompt:
Provide a low-pass filter design that achieves a high degree of noise rejection using only passives. The passband extends to 500 Hz with a maximum of 3.0 dB insertion loss. At 1 kHz, a minimum of 18.1 dB attenuation is required. No active components; use the fewest resistors and capacitors that satisfy all constraints.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a low-pass filter for a standard-impedance signal path. Pass signals through 8 kHz with under 3.0 dB loss. Provide at least 6.0 dB of attenuation at 16 kHz. Use only passive components and optimise for minimum BOM cost.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a low-pass filter for a high-impedance signal source with demanding stopband requirements. Frequencies at 5 kHz and below must be passed with less than 1.0 dB loss. At 50 kHz, the filter must deliver at least 40.0 dB of attenuation. Restrict components to resistors, capacitors, and at most one op-amp; minimise part count.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a low-pass filter for a sensor interface where the source impedance is high and the load is 1000 ohm. The passband must extend to 2 kHz with less than 0.5 dB loss. At 4 kHz, at least 6.0 dB of suppression is needed. Allow resistors, capacitors, and at most one op-amp; keep the design simple.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Create a low-pass filter for a high-impedance transducer requiring strong out-of-band rejection. Signals at 4 kHz must pass with less than 0.5 dB insertion loss. Signals at 20 kHz must be suppressed by at least 69.9 dB. Permitted components: resistors, capacitors, and one op-amp. Minimise component count.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a low-pass filter with sharp high-frequency rejection for an EMC application. Frequencies up to 1 kHz must be passed with <= 1.0 dB attenuation. Interference at 3 kHz must be reduced by a minimum of 47.7 dB. Use only resistors and capacitors, and favour the simplest topology that meets these figures.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Provide a low-pass filter design for a high-impedance signal source driving a 4700 ohm load. The insertion loss must be below 1.0 dB up to 1 kHz. A minimum of 12.0 dB attenuation is required at 4 kHz. Only one op-amp is permitted alongside resistors and capacitors; keep the BOM cost low.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Specify a low-pass RC network to remove high-frequency noise from a measurement line. Frequencies up to 2 kHz must be passed with <= 0.5 dB loss. The attenuation at 8 kHz must be >= 12.0 dB. The design should use the fewest passive components that satisfy these requirements.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.
